menu.title: IRC Control
settings.title: Settings
settings.pick_group: Send me to a group and pick it
senders.title:
Sender bots
A busy chat can spread its messages across several bots, each with its own rate limit.
senders.none: No extra sender bots yet. Add one to spread a busy chat's messages across more bots.
senders.add_prompt: Send a bot token from BotFather. Add that bot to this group as an admin first. The token is deleted after.
senders.invalid: That does not look like a bot token (it should be like 123456:ABC...). Send the token BotFather gave you.
addserver.name: Send a short name for this network (e.g. libera).
addserver.host: Send the server host (e.g. irc.libera.chat, or a .onion).
addserver.port: Send the port (6697 for TLS, 6667 for plaintext).
addserver.nick: Send the nick to use on this network.
addserver.auth: Auth method? Send: sasl, nickserv, or none.
addserver.sasl: Send the password for this account.
channels.none: No channels were returned - this network may not support channel listing.
discover.already_joined: ✓ You are already in this channel.
nick.invalid: That nick is not valid (ASCII letters/digits, no spaces). Send another.
nickserv.prompt: Send the NickServ password. It will be deleted after.
nickserv.register_prompt: Send a password (and optionally an email) to register your current nick with NickServ.
nickserv.registered: Registration sent to NickServ. Check the server buffer for its reply.
perform.title: {server} · commands on connect
perform.empty: No commands yet. Add one to run it automatically on every connect (for example a NickServ identify or a channel invite).
perform.add_prompt: Send one command to run on connect (for example /msg NickServ IDENTIFY yourpassword). It is added to the list.
ignores.title: Ignored nicks
ignores.add_prompt: Send a nick to ignore. Messages and events from it will be dropped.
names.title: Users in {channel}
onboard.needs_group: Create a supergroup with Topics enabled, add me as an admin, then send /usegroup inside it.
onboard.not_a_group: Send /usegroup inside the supergroup itself (with Topics enabled), not here in the private chat.
onboard.group_saved: Group set. Opening the control console here now.
onboard.ready: Already set up. Open the IRC Control topic.
help.intro: How this works Every IRC channel and every private chat you are in shows up here as its own topic. You read and reply inside a topic, and it goes straight to IRC; replies from IRC come back to the same topic. What appears where - A channel topic: everyone's messages, both directions. - A private topic: a one-on-one chat with one person. - The server topic (marked with a gear): connection status, the server welcome (MOTD), NickServ, and replies to commands like /whois or /list. - IRC Control: this console and its menus. Good to know - A line that mentions your nick is marked with a bell and pings you. - Your own messages are not echoed back to you. - Join, leave and quit notices are hidden by default; turn them on per server in its Settings. Tap a category below for the commands you can use.
help.cat.channels: Channels Type these with a leading / inside a channel topic or the console; the reply appears in that topic. Plain text (no /) is sent as a normal message. /join #channel - enter a channel (a topic is created for it). /part [#channel] [reason] - leave a channel (the current one if none given). /topic #channel [new topic] - show the channel subject, or set it. /names #channel - list who is in the channel. /list - list the channels on the server. /invite nick #channel - invite someone into a channel.
help.cat.users: Users Type these in a channel or the server topic; the reply appears there. /whois nick - who someone is: their name, host, channels, and idle time. /whowas nick - info about someone who just disconnected. /who #channel - list the people matching a channel or search. /msg nick text - send a private message (opens a private topic). /notice nick text - send a one-off notice. /ison nick ... - check which of these nicks are online.
help.cat.modes: Modes and moderation Modes are settings on a channel or a person, set by a channel operator. Type them in the channel topic. /mode #channel +o nick - make someone an operator (op): a channel manager who can kick, ban and set modes. /mode #channel +v nick - give voice: lets them talk in a moderated (+m) channel where others stay silent. /kick #channel nick [reason] - remove someone from the channel (they can rejoin unless banned). /mode #channel +b nick!*@* - ban: stop someone from joining or rejoining. Ban, then kick, to actually remove them. /mode #channel -b nick!*@* - lift a ban. A bare /mode #channel +b shows the ban list. Other channel modes: +m moderated (only ops and voiced talk), +i invite-only, +k a password, +l a user limit, +t only ops set the topic. You must be an operator to set these.
help.cat.messaging: Messaging Type these in the relevant topic. /msg nick|#channel text - send a message to a person or a channel. /notice target text - send a notice (a message clients never auto-reply to). /me action - send an action, shown as "* you wave". /query nick - open a private chat topic with someone. Replying (Telegram reply) to a channel message sends it as "nick: your text", so people see who you meant.
help.cat.server: Server and your session /nick newnick - change your nickname. /away [message] - mark yourself away, or clear it with no message. /quit [reason] - disconnect from the server. /msg NickServ REGISTER password - register your nick, then /msg NickServ IDENTIFY password to log in (some channels require a registered nick). /quote raw line - send a raw IRC line for anything not covered here.
help.cat.info: Server info /motd - the server message of the day. /lusers - how many users and servers are online. /version - the server software version. /time - the server local time. Any IRC command the network supports works here, even if it is not in this list.
addserver.tls:
Use TLS (an encrypted connection)?
Yes for a normal public server (usually port 6697). No for a plaintext or local test server (for example port 6667).
addserver.privacy:
Privacy for this server?
- None: a normal connection.
- Tor: hide your IP behind Tor.
- Anonymous: full anonymity (Tor, a scrubbed identity, remote DNS, and CTCP and file transfers off).
Choose None for a normal or local server.
confirm.remove: Remove {name}? This deletes the server and its topic mapping.
channel.left_prompt: You left this channel. What should happen to its topic?
channel.closed: Topic closed. It reopens if you rejoin the channel.
console.ambiguous_channel: {channel} is on more than one server. Run the command inside its own topic instead.
